title: Get started with timesfm
text: TimesFM’s inference code Python package, timesfm drives reticulate. install_timesfm() creates dedicated virtualenv (needs Python >= 3.10 tell can’t find one) installs PyTorch backend: first forecast downloads pretrained weights Hugging Face Hub caches ~/.cache/huggingface/. cached, add HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 ~/.Renviron skip Hub’s revalidation requests load straight disk.

title: Get started with timesfm
text: timesfm() takes observed series — numeric vector ts, oldest first — horizon: “Fitting” happens inside forward pass; nothing train. loaded model stays memory, repeated forecasts session fast. result holds point forecasts deciles forecast distribution: deciles give prediction intervals directly — q10 q90 bound 80% interval, q50 median:
code: fc <- timesfm(AirPassengers, horizon = 24) fc fc$mean               # point forecasts, length 24 fc$quantiles          # 24 x 9 matrix, columns q10 ... q90 plot(AirPassengers, xlim = c(1949, 1963)) lines(ts(fc$mean, start = c(1961, 1), frequency = 12), col = "blue") lines(ts(fc$quantiles[, "q10"], start = c(1961, 1), frequency = 12), lty = 2) lines(ts(fc$quantiles[, "q90"], start = c(1961, 1), frequency = 12), lty = 2)

what: When should you reach for TimesFM?
title: Get started with timesfm
text: TimesFM shines many heterogeneous series time model one, want strong baseline investing bespoke model. right tool : need covariates/regressors, holidays, hierarchical reconciliation — reach fable prophet. series longer-memory 1024 observations need forecasts beyond 256 steps. can’t ship Python runtime: inference always runs Python package.

what: R Interface to TimesFM, a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting
title: R Interface to TimesFM, a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting
text: goal timesfm make TimesFM, Google Research’s foundation model time series, easy use R. give observed series horizon; gives point forecasts forecast deciles training, model selection, tuning — pretrained model reads series context forecasts single forward pass. sibling tabfm package (zero-shot classification/regression tabular data), built way: thin reticulate wrapper around official Python package.

title: R Interface to TimesFM, a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting
text: can install development version timesfm like : , one time , install underlying Python package dedicated virtualenv:

title: R Interface to TimesFM, a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting
text: Pass numeric vector ts, oldest observation first, horizon 256 steps: first call loads pretrained weights (downloaded Hugging Face Hub cached); , forecasts session fast.
code: library(timesfm)  fc <- timesfm(AirPassengers, horizon = 24) fc  fc$mean                          # point forecasts fc$quantiles[, c("q10", "q90")]  # an 80% prediction interval

title: R Interface to TimesFM, a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting
text: pretrained weights cached first download, one optional line ~/.Renviron (Hugging Face account needed) makes cold starts noticeably faster: loads weights straight local cache instead revalidating Hugging Face Hub session’s first forecast. Unset whenever need download weights don’t yet.

title: R Interface to TimesFM, a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting
text: Forecasts require working Python installation; install_timesfm() sets everything via reticulate. Univariate series , context capped recent 1024 points, horizon capped 256 steps. TimesFM zero-shot: nothing tune. need covariates, hierarchical structure, bespoke accuracy one series, reach fable prophet.

what: Install the TimesFM Python package — install_timesfm
title: Install the TimesFM Python package — install_timesfm
text: install_timesfm() installs underlying TimesFM Python package (PyTorch backend) dedicated virtualenv via reticulate::py_install(). need run per machine.

title: Install the TimesFM Python package — install_timesfm
Install the TimesFM Python package — install_timesfm

what: timesfm: R Interface to 'TimesFM', a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting — timesfm-package
title: timesfm: R Interface to 'TimesFM', a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting — timesfm-package
text: thin wrapper around 'TimesFM' 'Python' package Google Research (https://github.com/google-research/timesfm). Provides zero-shot probabilistic forecasting univariate time series pretrained foundation model, training tuning.

title: timesfm: R Interface to 'TimesFM', a Foundation Model for Time Series Forecasting — timesfm-package
text: Maintainer: Matt O'Reilly fermoymatt@gmail.com Authors: Matt O'Reilly fermoymatt@gmail.com

what: Forecast a time series with TimesFM — timesfm
title: Forecast a time series with TimesFM — timesfm
text: timesfm() produces zero-shot probabilistic forecast univariate time series using pretrained TimesFM 2.5 (200M) foundation model. training step: model reads series context forecasts single forward pass. Loading pretrained weights slow part, happens per session model reused later calls.

title: Forecast a time series with TimesFM — timesfm
Forecast a time series with TimesFM — timesfm
text: y numeric vector ts object: observed series, oldest first, missing values (impute drop first). Series longer 1024 points used recent 1024. horizon Number future steps forecast, 1 256.

title: Forecast a time series with TimesFM — timesfm
text: object class "timesfm": list elements mean — numeric vector point forecasts, length horizon. quantiles — numeric matrix horizon rows columns q10 ... q90: deciles forecast distribution. horizon — forecast length.

title: Forecast a time series with TimesFM — timesfm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fc <- timesfm(AirPassengers, horizon = 24) fc  fc$mean fc$quantiles[, c("q10", "q90")]  # an 80% interval } # }

title: timesfm 0.1.0
text: Initial release. timesfm() produces zero-shot point decile forecasts univariate time series using Google Research’s TimesFM 2.5 (200M) foundation model. install_timesfm() performs one-time setup underlying Python package dedicated virtualenv, requiring Python >= 3.10.
